prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Between 13 and 18 May 2014 a low-pressure cyclone designated Tamara and Yvette affected a large area of Southeastern and Central Europe, causing floods and landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina suffered the greatest damage, as the rain was the heaviest in 120 years of recorded weather measurements. By 20 May, at least 62 people had died as a result of the flooding, and hundreds of thousands had been forced from their homes. Towns of Obrenovac in Serbia and Doboj in Bosnia and Herzegovina account for most victims, after being inundated by several-meter high waters from nearby rivers.Floodwaters caused over 2,000 landslides across Southeast Europe, spreading damage across many towns and villages. The rains activated torrents and mudslides, and subsequently several rivers in watersheds of Sava and Morava
B. Between 13 and 18 May 2014, a low-pressure cyclone named Tamara and Yvette impacted a significant portion of Southeastern and Central Europe, resulting in floods and landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina suffered the most damage, as the rainfall was the highest in 120 years. The majority of victims were located in the towns and villages of Obrenovac and Doboj in Bosnia and Herzegovina.
C. From May 13th to May 18th, 2014, a powerful low-pressure system, officially named Tamara and Yvette, impacted a substantial region of Southeastern and Central Europe, leading to widespread flooding and numerous landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina experienced the most significant destruction, as the rainfall recorded during this period was the most intense in 120 years of documented meteorological observations. By May 20th, a minimum of 62 individuals had tragically lost their lives due to the inundation, and hundreds of thousands of residents were displaced from their residences. The municipalities of Obrenovac in Serbia and Doboj in Bosnia and Herzegovina reported the highest number of casualties, having been submerged by several-meter-deep waters originating from adjacent rivers. The expansive floodwaters triggered more than 2,000 landslides throughout Southeast Europe, extending the devastation to numerous communities and rural settlements. The torrential downpours activated flash floods and mudflows, which subsequently affected multiple rivers within the drainage basins of the Sava and Morava.

Answer:

options.A: Between 13 and 18 May 2014 a low-pressure cyclone designated Tamara and Yvette affected a large area of Southeastern and Central Europe, causing floods and landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina suffered the greatest damage, as the rain was the heaviest in 120 years of recorded weather measurements. By 20 May, at least 62 people had died as a result of the flooding, and hundreds of thousands had been forced from their homes. Towns of Obrenovac in Serbia and Doboj in Bosnia and Herzegovina account for most victims, after being inundated by several-meter high waters from nearby rivers.Floodwaters caused over 2,000 landslides across Southeast Europe, spreading damage across many towns and villages. The rains activated torrents and mudslides, and subsequently several rivers in watersheds of Sava and Morava
options.B: Between 13 and 18 May 2014, a low-pressure cyclone named Tamara and Yvette impacted a significant portion of Southeastern and Central Europe, resulting in floods and landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina suffered the most damage, as the rainfall was the highest in 120 years. The majority of victims were located in the towns and villages of Obrenovac and Doboj in Bosnia and Herzegovina.
options.C: From May 13th to May 18th, 2014, a powerful low-pressure system, officially named Tamara and Yvette, impacted a substantial region of Southeastern and Central Europe, leading to widespread flooding and numerous landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina experienced the most significant destruction, as the rainfall recorded during this period was the most intense in 120 years of documented meteorological observations. By May 20th, a minimum of 62 individuals had tragically lost their lives due to the inundation, and hundreds of thousands of residents were displaced from their residences. The municipalities of Obrenovac in Serbia and Doboj in Bosnia and Herzegovina reported the highest number of casualties, having been submerged by several-meter-deep waters originating from adjacent rivers. The expansive floodwaters triggered more than 2,000 landslides throughout Southeast Europe, extending the devastation to numerous communities and rural settlements. The torrential downpours activated flash floods and mudflows, which subsequently affected multiple rivers within the drainage basins of the Sava and Morava.
